source.title: Care Ethics (Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy entry, summarising Gilligan, Noddings, Tronto, Held, Kittay).
source.venue: Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy (peer-reviewed reference work)

confidence_rationale: INV-043 is explicitly philosophical (architecture necessary but not sufficient; received caregiving as developmental requirement for ethical agency). Care ethics, especially Noddings, makes the most direct philosophical case for INV-043's structural commitment: ethical action originates in the memory of being cared-for. The IEP entry is a peer-reviewed reference work that consolidates Noddings, Gilligan, Tronto, Held, Kittay -- a useful single anchor. Confidence is bounded by (a) the philosophical-not-empirical nature of the source and (b) substantive critiques of care ethics (essentialism, parochialism) that bound how strongly INV-043 can lean on this tradition.
failure_signatures: If INV-043's architectural-necessary-not-sufficient framing were wrong, care ethics' developmental-relational core could not provide it any philosophical traction. The traction is real (Noddings explicitly grounds ethical action in 'the memory of being cared-for'), but care ethics' own internal critiques (essentialism, parochialism) constrain how much weight INV-043 can put on it.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Care ethics (Gilligan 1982, Noddings 1984/2013, Tronto, Held, Kittay) holds that ethics emerges from concrete relationships of dependency and vulnerability, particularly developmental caregiver-receiver relations. Noddings argues that ethical action originates in 'the memory of being cared-for' that generates an ideal self motivating moral response. Humans are inherently relational beings whose ethical capacities develop through experiences of being cared for.
mapping.ree_translation: INV-043 is essentially the REE-substrate version of care ethics' developmental claim: the architecture provides the capacity, but the resolution toward ethical agency requires the substrate to receive caregiving inputs during a developmental window. Noddings's 'memory of being cared-for' is the substrate-level memory anchor; INV-043's architectural-resolution claim is its REE realisation. The philosophical lineage gives INV-043 the strongest available conceptual support, even though it cannot empirically test it.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Care ethics is contested philosophical territory. Major critiques include essentialism (assuming gendered or universal caring patterns), parochialism (proximity bias may license favouritism over distant others), and 'slave morality' worries (valorising self-sacrificial caring without addressing systemic injustice). INV-043 should not absorb these problems wholesale -- it can lean on the developmental-substrate insight without committing to the more contested feminist or political-care-ethics extensions. The IEP article is a secondary reference work, not primary philosophical argument; for governance weight, primary Noddings/Gilligan/Held citations would carry more authority.
mapping.source_context: Peer-reviewed encyclopedia entry summarising the care ethics tradition from Gilligan and Noddings through Tronto, Held, Kittay; cited extensively in moral psychology and philosophy of education.

confidence_components.notes: IEP is peer-reviewed reference but secondary; primary Noddings citations would be stronger. Mapping fidelity is high because care ethics' core thesis is essentially the philosophical version of INV-043. Transfer risk is the philosophical-to-substrate translation cost.
